description: This lecture discusses the Repeated Prisoner's Dilemma, exploring why cooperation can occur despite incentives to defect. It examines scenarios where short-term gains from defection are outweighed by long-term costs, emphasizing the importance of reputation in transactions. The lecture also introduces the concept of backward induction and proposes a model with a random number of stages to better reflect real-world cooperation.
topic_summary: Incentives in the Repeated Prisoner's Dilemma and the dynamics of cooperation versus defection, including the implications of backward induction and a model with uncertain stage lengths.
